title: T-009 — quality_audit_log table + audit triggers on quality_holds
prompt:
# T-009 — quality_audit_log + audit triggers

PRD: docs/prd/09-QUALITY-PRD.md §5.2 quality_audit_log DDL, §9.1 audit_hold_change trigger, §13.3 audit log completeness
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira

## Goal
Create `quality_audit_log` table with retention_until and indexes per §5.2, plus the audit_hold_change trigger function and triggers on quality_holds (INSERT + UPDATE) per §9.1.

## Implementation contract
1. Drizzle migration `packages/db/migrations/0103_quality_audit_log.sql` creates quality_audit_log per §5.2 verbatim (BIGSERIAL PK, `org_id uuid NOT NULL`, occurred_at, table_name, record_id, operation, old_data, new_data, changed_fields, user_id, session_id, change_reason, ip_address, user_agent, request_id, retention_until GENERATED) plus idx_audit_record and idx_audit_user indexes; ENABLE+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY with policy `quality_audit_log_org_context` USING (`org_id = app.current_org_id()`) WITH CHECK (`org_id = app.current_org_id()`) — the foundation function from `packages/db/migrations/002-rls-baseline.sql`; never `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` GUC.
2. CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION raise_immutable_error() per §9.1.
3. CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION audit_hold_change() per §9.1: reads org via `app.current_org_id()` (foundation function) for the audit row's `org_id` and writes actor `user_id` from a session GUC (e.g. `current_setting('app.current_user_id', true)`); changed_fields computed via jsonb_each diff. `request_id` may use a GUC via `current_setting('app.request_id', true)`. NEVER use `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` — those have been replaced by the function.
4. CREATE TRIGGER audit_hold_insert AFTER INSERT and audit_hold_update AFTER UPDATE on quality_holds FOR EACH ROW.

## Files
- packages/db/migrations/0103_quality_audit_log.sql [create]
- packages/db/__tests__/quality-audit-log.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given migration 0103 applied, when \d quality_audit_log runs, then all §5.2 columns exist (operation, old_data jsonb, new_data jsonb, changed_fields text[], retention_until GENERATED), org_id NOT NULL, and RLS+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY enabled
2. Given the connection as `app_user` has called `app.set_org_context(session_token, org_id)` and `SET LOCAL app.current_user_id = '<uuid>'`, when a quality_holds INSERT fires the trigger, then exactly 1 quality_audit_log row is appended with operation='INSERT', new_data set, old_data NULL, user_id matching the GUC, and org_id matching `app.current_org_id()`
3. Given an existing quality_holds row, when UPDATE changes hold_status from 'open' to 'released', then 1 quality_audit_log row is appended with operation='UPDATE', changed_fields containing 'hold_status' and 'released_at'
4. Given pg_policies and trigger function source are inspected, when grep'd, then they reference `app.current_org_id()` and contain no `current_setting('app.tenant_id')` or `current_setting('app.current_org_id')` GUC reads

pipeline_inputs.description: Create quality_audit_log table (org_id-scoped, foundation-app.current_org_id() RLS) + raise_immutable_error + audit_hold_change function + INSERT/UPDATE triggers on quality_holds.
pipeline_inputs.details: Migration 0103 emits quality_audit_log DDL per §5.2 verbatim with `org_id uuid NOT NULL` (Wave0 v4.3) and retention_until GENERATED 7y. Functions raise_immutable_error (RAISE EXCEPTION) and audit_hold_change (read org via `app.current_org_id()` for the audit row's org_id; read actor via `current_setting('app.current_user_id', true)`; compute changed_fields via jsonb_each; INSERT into log). Triggers AFTER INSERT and AFTER UPDATE on quality_holds. ENABLE+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY with policy `quality_audit_log_org_context` USING/WITH CHECK `org_id = app.current_org_id()` on quality_audit_log itself. RLS uses foundation `app.current_org_id()` function (Wave0 decision; see `_meta/audits/2026-05-14-architecture-and-cross-cutting-gaps.md` §Tenant-context).
out_of_scope: Do not add triggers on quality_inspections / specs / NCR / monitoring here (T-040 covers cross-table triggers)
Do not implement immutability triggers on signed records (T-040)
acceptance_criteria: Given migration 0103 applied, when \d quality_audit_log runs, then all §5.2 columns exist (operation, old_data jsonb, new_data jsonb, changed_fields text[], retention_until GENERATED), org_id NOT NULL, RLS+FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY enabled

Given app_user has called app.set_org_context(session_token, org_id) and SET LOCAL app.current_user_id = '<uuid>', when a quality_holds INSERT fires the trigger, then exactly 1 quality_audit_log row appears with operation='INSERT', new_data populated, old_data NULL, user_id matching the GUC, and org_id matching app.current_org_id()

Given an existing quality_holds row, when UPDATE flips hold_status open->released, then 1 audit row is appended with operation='UPDATE' and changed_fields containing both 'hold_status' and 'released_at'

Given pg_policies and trigger function source are inspected, when grep'd, then they reference app.current_org_id() and contain no current_setting('app.tenant_id') or current_setting('app.current_org_id') GUC reads
test_strategy: RED: write packages/db/__tests__/quality-audit-log.test.ts asserting schema, INSERT trigger (via app.set_org_context + SET LOCAL app.current_user_id as app_user), UPDATE diff capture, and pg_policies references app.current_org_id() before migration exists
Run 'pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test quality-audit-log'
risk_red_lines: Do not read current_setting('app.tenant_id') or current_setting('app.current_org_id') directly — use the foundation app.current_org_id() function so the NULL-safe setter contract is preserved.
Do not use tenant_id as the business-scope column; per Wave0 v4.3 lock it is org_id.
Do not allow RLS / FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY bypass on the audit log itself
Do not omit retention_until GENERATED 7y column (BRCGS Issue 10)
Do not allow triggers to swallow errors silently (RAISE on missing app.current_user_id)
